response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and web developer with strong software craftsmanship, but the role requires deep hardware-level expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design. His background is predominantly web, mobile, and application development; the closest relevant experience (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ at National Instruments) is dated and far from silicon architecture or deep-learning workload optimization. There is no evidence of GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, DNN training/inference optimization, or performance modeling of hardware, making him a poor fit for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec) using asynchronous I/O and caching in DLang
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, or accelerator performance-optimization background
Recent career trajectory is full-stack product development, not principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN workload experience
